mnemonics: Normal Forms are Like Dating Rules: 1NF (Don't be messy), 2NF (Be fully committed), 3NF (No talking behind backs)
keyPoints: Normalization is the process of organizing data to reduce redundancy and improve data integrity.
A functional dependency (X → Y) means the value of Y is determined by the value of X.
Decomposition must be lossless to ensure no information is lost when splitting tables.
commonMistakes: Confusing partial dependency (depends on part of a composite key) with transitive dependency (depends on a non-key attribute).
Assuming more normalization is always better, without considering the performance cost of excessive joins.
Forgetting to ensure that a decomposition is dependency-preserving, which is crucial for maintaining integrity constraints.
